title: cassia xanthophylla
intro: Cassia xanthophylla, also known as the Australian senna, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its yellow flowers and seeds. The medicinal properties of Cassia xanthophylla have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, due to its purgative and laxative effects. The health benefits of this plant include relieving constipation, reducing inflammation, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. Cassia xanthophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and overall well-being.
